title: Aristophanis Lysistrata
summaries: "Aristophanis Lysistrata" by Aristophanes is an ancient Greek comedy first staged in 411 BCE. When the Peloponnesian War drags on endlessly, Lysistrata devises a bold plan: women from warring Greek city-states will withhold sex from their husbands until peace is negotiated. The women seize the Acropolis and its treasury, igniting a battle between the sexes. As men grow desperate and tensions escalate, this subversive comedy explores power, desire, and women's agency in a male-dominated society. (This is an automatically generated summary.)

translators.name: Brunck, Rich. Fr. Phil. (Richard François Philippe)

subjects: Greece -- History -- Peloponnesian War, 431-404 B.C. -- Drama
